# `liber:network@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:network` package (`network.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem network package - networking, sockets, and TCP services.

## Imports

- `error` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)

## Types

### record `ipv4-addr`

A 32-bit IPv4 address as four octets - the canonical typed network address, never a parsed string on the wire (a textual form is only ever a rendering of these octets).

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `a` | `u8` |  |
| `b` | `u8` |  |
| `c` | `u8` |  |
| `d` | `u8` |  |

### record `endpoint`

A transport endpoint: an IPv4 address and a port.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `addr` | `ipv4-addr` |  |
| `port` | `u16` |  |

### record `neighbor`

A neighbor-cache entry: an on-link address and the MAC (6 bytes) it resolved to.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `addr` | `ipv4-addr` |  |
| `mac` | `list<u8>` |  |

### record `net-info`

The interface's L3 state: our address, our MAC (6 bytes), the interface MTU, the gateway, and the neighbor cache - the typed form of what the `ip` / `net` command renders.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `addr` | `ipv4-addr` |  |
| `mac` | `list<u8>` |  |
| `mtu` | `u16` |  |
| `gateway` | `ipv4-addr` |  |
| `neighbors` | `list<neighbor>` |  |

### record `net-capacity`

The NetworkService pool utilization: how many client, socket and listener channels the serve loop currently stands on, and how many live TCP connections the stack holds. Every pool grows on demand (the domain's handle budget is the only ceiling), so these are the live counts `ss` reports and the graph folds in - observability, not a cap.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `clients` | `u32` |  |
| `sockets` | `u32` |  |
| `listeners` | `u32` |  |
| `connections` | `u32` |  |

### enum `ping-status`

The outcome of a ping: a reply arrived, it timed out, or the target was unreachable (no route / no ARP).

| case | ordinal | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `reply` | 0 |  |
| `timeout` | 1 |  |
| `unreachable` | 2 |  |

### record `ping-reply`

The result of one ping echo: the outcome, the reply packet's IP TTL, and the measured round-trip time in microseconds. `ttl` and `rtt-us` are meaningful only when `status` is `reply` (zero otherwise).

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `ping-status` |  |
| `ttl` | `u8` |  |
| `rtt-us` | `u32` |  |

### record `tcp-request`

A one-shot TCP exchange: connect to `ep`, send `request`, and read the response.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ep` | `endpoint` |  |
| `request` | `list<u8>` |  |

### enum `sock-state`

A live socket's TCP state - the typed form of the state column `ss` prints.

| case | ordinal | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `closed` | 0 |  |
| `syn-sent` | 1 |  |
| `syn-rcvd` | 2 |  |
| `established` | 3 |  |
| `fin-wait` | 4 |  |
| `listen` | 5 |  |

### record `sock-info`

A live socket in the NetworkService table: its local port, the remote endpoint it talks to (zeros for a listening socket), and its TCP state - the typed form of one row `ss` lists.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `local-port` | `u16` |  |
| `remote` | `endpoint` |  |
| `state` | `sock-state` |  |

### record `chunk`

One chunk of received socket data, the element of the `socket.recv` stream.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data` | `list<u8>` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `network`

The NetworkService contract: the interface state, name resolution, ping, a one-shot TCP fetch, `connect` - which opens a TCP connection and hands the client a socket as a capability (the channel a `socket` interface is served on) - and `listen`, which opens a listening socket for inbound connections. `connect` / `listen` return their channel out-of-band as a `handle<channel>`; the client wraps it in a `socket` / `listener` client. So the network is reachable through the typed API like every other service.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `info` | `info() -> result<net-info, error>` |  |
| 2 | `resolve` | `resolve(name: string) -> result<ipv4-addr, error>` |  |
| 3 | `ping` | `ping(addr: ipv4-addr) -> result<ping-reply, error>` |  |
| 4 | `fetch` | `fetch(req: tcp-request) -> result<list<u8>, error>` |  |
| 5 | `connect` | `connect(ep: endpoint) -> result<handle<channel>, error>` |  |
| 6 | `open` | `open() -> result<handle<channel>, error>` |  |
| 7 | `listen` | `listen(port: u16) -> result<handle<channel>, error>` |  |
| 8 | `sockets` | `sockets() -> result<list<sock-info>, error>` |  |
| 9 | `sntp` | `sntp(server: ipv4-addr) -> result<u64, error>` |  |
| 10 | `capacity` | `capacity() -> result<net-capacity, error>` |  |

### interface `socket`

A connected TCP socket, served on the channel `network.connect` hands back as a capability: send bytes (passed zero-copy as a `buffer` - a shared-memory handle, so the payload never crosses the channel), receive incoming bytes as a wait-drained event stream of chunks (the producer closes when the peer closes - end of stream), and close. The socket is the canonical object; each net tool asks NetworkService for the socket it needs and talks this contract.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `send` | `send(data: buffer) -> result<u32, error>` |  |
| 2 | `recv` | `recv() -> stream<chunk>` |  |
| 3 | `close` | `close() -> result<unit, error>` |  |

### interface `listener`

A listening socket from `network.listen`, served on the channel it hands back. `accept` waits until an inbound connection completes its handshake, then hands it back as a `socket` capability (the channel a `socket` interface is served on) - the passive-open counterpart to `network.connect`. The server calls `accept` in a loop, each call yielding the next connection.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `accept` | `accept() -> result<handle<channel>, error>` |  |
